subsection.heading: General Limitations.—
subsection.chapeau: Except as provided in subsection (b) of this section and section 47530 of this title, a person may operate a civil subsonic turbojet aircraft with a maximum weight of more than 75,000 pounds that is imported into the United States after November 4, 1990, only if the aircraft—

paragraph.content: complies with the stage 3 noise levels; or
was purchased by the person importing the aircraft into the United States under a legally binding contract made before November 5, 1990.

subsection.heading: Exemptions.—
subsection.content: The Secretary of Transportation may provide an exemption from subsection (a) of this section to permit a person to obtain modifications to an aircraft to meet the stage 3 noise levels.

subsection.heading: Aircraft Deemed Not Imported.—
subsection.chapeau: In this section, an aircraft is deemed not to have been imported into the United States if the aircraft—

subparagraph.content: a corporation, trust, or partnership organized under the laws of the United States or a State (including the District of Columbia);
an individual who is a citizen of the United States; or
an entity that is owned or controlled by a corporation, trust, partnership, or individual described in subclause (A) or (B) of this clause; and

paragraph.content: enters the United States not later than 6 months after the expiration of a lease agreement (including any extension) between an owner described in clause (1) of this subsection and a foreign carrier.

text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-left:1px solid black; padding-left: 2pt;
